# Path-of-Funds Flow — TEMPLATE

> **How to use:** Trace the invested capital **hop by hop**, from the account where the funds
> originated to the project's escrow / new commercial enterprise (NCE). Every hop needs a
> document that evidences it. Reviewers reject filings when a step in the chain is
> unexplained, so list **every** intermediary — including currency conversion and any
> third-party transfer. Replace each `[[FILL: ...]]` and save your completed version as
> `Path_of_Funds.md` in this folder (don't overwrite this template).
>
> **Privacy:** keep full account numbers out of this file — a masked reference (e.g.,
> "Bank A account ending NNNN") is enough to map the chain. The skills do not read the filled
> contents; they only confirm it exists and has no remaining `[[FILL: ...]]` placeholders.

## Transfer chain

| Step | Date | From (party / account ref) | To (party / account ref) | Evidence document (filename) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [[FILL]] | [[FILL: origin account]] | [[FILL]] | [[FILL]] |
| 2 | [[FILL]] | [[FILL]] | [[FILL]] | [[FILL]] |
| 3 | [[FILL]] | [[FILL]] | [[FILL: escrow / NCE]] | [[FILL]] |

> Add or remove rows so the chain runs unbroken from origin to the project.

## Intermediaries & conversions
Explain any step that isn't a simple bank-to-bank transfer (currency exchange house, family
member's account, third-party payer, FX approval).

> [[FILL: list each intermediary and why the funds passed through it; attach the supporting doc]]

## Regulatory / FX approvals
If your jurisdiction requires approval to move funds abroad, note it and attach the approval.

> [[FILL: jurisdiction-specific FX/remittance approvals, or "n/a" with a one-line reason]]

---

> Organizational template, **not legal advice**. Your attorney decides what trail evidence is
> sufficient for your specific filing.
